sglang.title: Fix oom issues with fp8 for llama
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
The current code stores a copy of params_dict by self.param_dict = dict(self.named_parameters()). This saves a reference to the old weights and it will make the fp16 weights unable to be released after quantization. This bug greatly increases memory usage and harms performance.
This PR fixes this bu

sglang.title: Fuse top_k and top_k in the sampler
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt: This can reduce the memory usage and make it faster.

sglang.title: [Event] Add public meeting invite to README
sglang.author: Ying1123

vllm.title: [Misc] Add argument to disable FastAPI docs
vllm.author: Jeffwan
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR is to add a new argument option to disable the /openapi.json, /docs and /redoc. My company doesn't allow using such endpoints in production environments. A new argument is added to disable the endpoint
reference doc: https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/metadata/
vllm serve facebook/opt-125

vllm.title: [CI/Build] Avoid CUDA initialization
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR removes the vast majority of torch.cuda.is_available and torch.cuda.get_device_capability calls, thus avoiding CUDA re-initialization errors when vLLM is run multiple times in tests. The only remaining ones are in the files that don't import vLLM.

vllm.title: [CI/Build] Update Ruff version
vllm.author: aarnphm
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Updated ruff to 0.6.5, possible to also run ruff check
Signed-off-by: Aaron Pham contact@aarnphm.xyz

vllm.title: [Core][Bugfix][Perf] Introduce `MQLLMEngine` to avoid `asyncio` OH
vllm.author: alexm-redhat
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
SUMMARY:

Removed almost all the overhead from the OpenAI server, but still saw significant slowdown running in AsyncLLMEngine rather than LLMEngine on H100, including when we ran "headless" (e.g. with no uvicorn server).
NOTE: performance varies by quality of the CPU. Impact of asyncio on DGX is mu

vllm.title: [Core] zmq: bind only to 127.0.0.1 for local-only usage
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt: When running with --tensor-parallel-size greater than 1, I noticed that the zmq socket opened for broadcasting to workers was listening on all interfaces. This change makes both the listening-side and (XPUB) and the receiving side (SUB) use 127.0.0.1 instead of * when we know the usage is local-only

vllm.title: [Kernel] Change interface to Mamba selective_state_update for continuous batching
vllm.author: tlrmchlsmth
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
See also #8012
Currently, the state passed to selective_state_update must be represented a single tensor. This leads to difficulties with the management of the state for Jamba models in vLLM. This is because with continuous batching, the items in the current batch have arrived at different times, an

vllm.title: [BugFix] Nonzero exit code if MQLLMEngine startup fails
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
It's better to indicate/propagate engine startup failure via exception rather than the build_async_engine_client_from_engine_args context manager yielding None in this case.
The exception will propagate to the top of the stack and cause nonzero exit code from the main process (which is what we want)

vllm.title: [Bugfix] add `dead_error` property to engine client
vllm.author: joerunde
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This should fix a stack trace I'm now seeing with the new MQLLMEngine:
INFO:     10.131.3.24:51916 - "POST /v1/completions HTTP/1.1" 500 Internal Server Error
ERROR:    Exception in ASGI application
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/workspace/my-vllm/lib64/python3.11/site-packages/uvicorn/

vllm.title: [Kernel] Remove marlin moe templating on thread_m_blocks
vllm.author: tlrmchlsmth
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Reduce Marlin MoE binary size and compile time by removing the template parameter thread_m_blocks in MarlinMoE. On current main, the MarlinMoE fn is completely ignoring thread_m_blocks, so deleting the template parameter is a free win.
This PR:
Wheel dist/vllm-0.6.1.post2+cu124-cp38-abi3-linux_x86_6

vllm.title: [Bugfix] [Encoder-Decoder] Bugfix for encoder specific metadata construction during decode of encoder-decoder models.
vllm.author: sroy745
vllm.bodyExcerpt: In the encoder decoder model runner when constructing the cross-attention block table and encoder-seq length metadata we assume that there is only one sequence per sequence group. However this assumption will not hold when we are using beam search. In this pr we replicate the cross-attention block t
